stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 157.8 % and a standard deviation of 9.7 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.4% and 4.6%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 157.8 %. After correcting for the 3.0% bias: ≈ 153.2 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.4%-4.6%) and sampling variability: [144.8, 161.7]. Point estimate: 153.2 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
